title: Archive Terrace Module
description: A stepped architectural module that stores remembered scenes as physical volumes. The module combines a broad ceremonial stair, recessed archive cells, projecting reading balconies, and a luminous indexing spine. Multiple modules can terrace across a slope, rotate around a basin, or stack into a fragmented amphitheater without losing a clear public approach.
narrativeFunction: Archive terraces translate memory storage into traversable civic architecture. They provide mid-ground mass, platforms for lanterns and figures, repeated scale cues, and a transition between intimate memory anchors and the enormous shell systems.

orientation.originMeaning: The origin sits at the center of the lowest front step on ground level so snapping modules preserves terrace rise, public orientation, and walkable alignment.

silhouette.primary: A broad three-step wedge rising toward the rear, with one tall off-center indexing spine breaking an otherwise horizontal skyline.
silhouette.secondary: Deep archive recesses create a rhythm of dark vertical slots while two projecting reading balconies interrupt the stepped mass at different heights.
silhouette.tertiary: Shelf lips, inscription bands, lantern niches, drainage channels, book-like memory volumes, repair blocks, rails, and access ladders concentrate detail along reachable edges.
silhouette.negativeSpace: Archive recesses are true deep voids rather than dark decals. The gaps below reading balconies and the narrow cut beside the indexing spine remain essential secondary spaces.
silhouette.recognitionTest: From a distance the module reads as a stepped archive or fragmented amphitheater with one vertical index marker, never as a generic staircase, ziggurat, or solid retaining wall.

materials.surfaceDescription: Dark violet-gray basalt with broad worn planes, chipped stair noses, mineral veining, patched blocks, and polished paths where visitors repeatedly walked.
Aged bronze with blackened recesses, crisp engraved catalog lines, polished hand-contact surfaces, and occasional turquoise oxidation.
Translucent book-like blocks in amber, muted rose, and dim violet with internal depth and moving bands rather than flat glowing panels.

components.role: Primary stepped stone body establishing the module footprint and public approach.
components.detail: Carve a broad public stair into the forward half, preserve worn load-bearing edges, and introduce two irregular repair patches without disrupting walkability.

components.role: Two receding upper platforms producing the stepped archive silhouette.
components.detail: Tier faces contain deep cell cuts and never align perfectly with the lower platform; rear edges step laterally by several centimeters.

components.role: Deep framed openings containing individually varied memory volumes.
components.detail: Each cell has a bronze frame, uneven shelf depth, and zero to four stored volumes with different size, color, and emission intensity.

components.role: Tall off-center catalog marker and vertical circulation landmark.
components.detail: Taper the spine upward, split its front face with a luminous catalog channel, and model engraved level bands and one service hatch.

components.role: Projecting ledges for figures, reading stands, and lantern clusters.
components.detail: Each balcony uses two underside ribs, one solid parapet section, one open rail section, and a visible drainage slot.

assemblyOrder: Insert varied memory volumes and bronze frames
Add index spine and light channel
Attach reading balconies, rails, niches, and access details
deformationRules: Stair and platform planes remain planar, but exposed front edges may erode inward by up to eight centimeters in irregular patches.
The indexing spine leans two degrees away from the public approach and narrows twelve percent toward the top.
Reading balconies project at slightly different angles so repeated modules do not create perfectly parallel bands.
variationRules: Archive-cell occupancy varies from thirty to ninety percent while every module retains at least two completely dark empty cells.
Memory-volume emission alternates warm amber, muted rose, and dim violet without creating a regular checkerboard.
Modules may mirror or rotate around a basin, but the indexing spine remains on an exterior-facing side.

topology.target: Game-ready modular architecture with separate walkable terraces, cell inserts, balcony assemblies, index spine, and replaceable memory-volume props.
topology.bevel: Use broad worn stone bevels on public edges, tighter bronze bevels on frames and rails, and intentionally chipped sections only where they preserve collision continuity.
topology.subdivision: No global subdivision. Use local smoothing only on worn stone repairs and rounded rail grips while preserving architectural planes.
topology.normals: Weighted normals keep large terrace planes calm and make stair noses, balcony ribs, and index-spine cuts carry controlled highlights.
topology.uv: Use a shared basalt material scale, bronze trim sheet, unique emissive UVs for catalog marks, and reusable memory-volume atlas regions.

animation.motionCharacter: Architecture remains fixed. Soft bands move inside stored volumes at different rates, the index spine responds in delayed catalog pulses, and balcony lanterns flicker independently.
constraints: No structural object moves during idle animation.
Emission remains below twice the base intensity.
Empty archive cells stay fully dark.

collision.description: Use one stepped compound collider matching walkable tiers plus simplified balcony boxes. Archive recesses, rails, memory volumes, and small niches remain non-colliding.

sockets.purpose: Horizontal connection to an adjacent terrace module.
Horizontal connection to an adjacent terrace module.
Placement surface for a smaller rotated archive module or hero prop.
Navigation and camera target at the lower stair approach.

orthographicGuidance.front: Show three stepped levels, deep archive slots, two uneven balconies, and the indexing spine offset to the viewer's right.
orthographicGuidance.side: Make the wedge profile, terrace depth, balcony projection, and slight rear lean of the spine legible.
orthographicGuidance.top: Reveal the public stair approach, irregular cell depths, balcony footprints, and left/right modular snapping edges.
orthographicGuidance.threeQuarter: Use a low public-approach angle that exposes worn stair surfaces, glowing cell interiors, underside balcony structure, and the spine against open sky.
orthographicGuidance.scaleCue: Include one human on the lower stair and another reading on an upper balcony.

validation: The module contains three readable elevation levels and one off-center index spine.
At least six archive cells have visibly modeled depth from the hero view.
Two reading balconies project from different levels and do not align vertically.
The lower stair remains continuously walkable using the simplified collision shape.
Memory-volume emission varies without flattening the dark stone architecture.
LOD2 preserves the stepped mass, index spine, and four luminous openings.
Left and right sockets align when two default modules are placed adjacent without overlap.
